topic: Telluric ~12 Hz Literature Peak — change-detected re-review (post-c3-patch verification + grammatical-bug catch)
change_summary: detail_evidence_length grew from 592 (c3) to 850 (current) — +43.6% drift driven by ISS-1866 patch adding the MT/Schumann bridge sentence (the exact text recommended in c3 hole #2). detail_verdict_length nominally drifted -2.5% (1114 → 1086) due to ISS-1867 source-list replacement. Pre-tldr-schema fingerprint, compared on common fields only; sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-09T01:10Z (76h old, within 168h fallback threshold). obe_skipped=9 (WIN-063/066/043/006/033/028/004/050/017 — all already re-reviewed since the May-9 audit ran). WIN-009 was head of the deep tail after OBE-skip.
our_argument_summary: Verdict (Refuted by Data) and core scientific argument (12 Hz is an inter-harmonic minimum; 14.3 Hz first harmonic falsifies any cutoff or peak in the inter-harmonic gap) are unchanged from c3. The c3 review surfaced 4 holes (#1 hardcoded-no-fetch phrasing, #2 MT/Schumann bridge, #3 boilerplate source list, #4 add quantitative harmonic line). Holes #1, #2, #3 were converted to ISS-1865/1866/1867 and patched on 2026-05-06 (see monitor/decisions/applied-patches/patches-2026-05-06T07-00-WIN009-WIN050.json). Hole #4 was not opened as an issue and remains unaddressed. This c4 review verifies the three landed patches and catches a new bug introduced by the ISS-1867 implementation.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3. Dome's WIN-009 entry in raw-text/02-wins.txt still reads 'Multiple independent MT literature sources. Telluric ~12 Hz peak consistent across independent datasets.' No formula, no derivation, no specific MT publication cited by the dome.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged. Display-only literature citation; no on-site computation. monitor.py not present in this raw-text snapshot.
data_sources_used: unspecified MT literature (no DOIs cited by the dome)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c3 — 'consistent across independent datasets' phrasing remains unfalsifiable absent specific datasets/thresholds.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c3. raw-text/02-wins.txt records WIN-009 as a literature-citation claim; no API/URL fetch behind it. Tag retained from prior cycles; monitor.py not in this snapshot.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Schumann inter-harmonic power minima are textbook spherical-cavity physics (Chave & Jones, 2012, DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138). The dome relabels a trough as a peak; the underlying spectrum is the same.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Schumann fundamental and harmonics catalogued for decades (Balser & Wagner 1960; Sentman 1995). Any ~12 Hz MT feature is observable and indexed well before the dome model. Neither temporal-impossibility nor provable-unawareness carve-out applies.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. No formula on the dome site derives 12 Hz from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any aetheric parameter. The dome's flat-disc cavity formulas yield 11.79 Hz only when D is silently set to Earth's diameter (a globe parameter) — they do not produce 12 Hz from dome geometry alone.

holes_found.description: Ungrammatical text introduced by the ISS-1867 patch. detail_verdict_text currently reads: 'The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset published MT and ELF spectral literature that was publicly available...' Two noun phrases ('a field-specific published dataset' and 'published MT and ELF spectral literature') are concatenated with no connector or punctuation. This is user-visible prose in the verdict block, which renders inside the WIN-009 detail panel on the public site. Cross-checking the other 18 WINs that carry the post-hoc paragraph (grep across data/wins.json), 17 of them retain the canonical parenthetical pattern 'a field-specific published dataset (<example>) that was publicly available' — e.g., WIN-008 (the immediately adjacent WIN, which WIN-009 cross-links to): 'a field-specific published dataset (published MT and ELF spectral literature) that was publicly available...'. The ISS-1867 patch find/replace stripped the parentheses along with the IGRF/NASA boilerplate, leaving the replacement text grammatically dangling. The bug is local and load-bearing for credibility: a reader who clicks through WIN-008 → WIN-009 (the two WINs share a verdict cluster and explicitly cross-reference) will see the same paragraph rendered correctly in one and broken in the other.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-patch detail_verdict_text on WIN-009. Find: 'a field-specific published dataset published MT and ELF spectral literature'. Replace: 'a field-specific published dataset (published MT and ELF spectral literature)'. This matches WIN-008's phrasing exactly and is the minimum change to restore grammaticality. Decider should open an ISS, generate the patch, self-apply (this is a clear minor patch), then push WIN-009 back for verification.

holes_found.description: c3 hole #4 carry-over — still unaddressed. c3 recommended adding a single quantitative line to detail_evidence summarizing the harmonic spectrum: 'measured Schumann harmonics: 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz — the existence of all four above 11.7 Hz directly falsifies any cutoff or peak in the inter-harmonic gap.' This makes WIN-009 standalone-readable without requiring the WIN-008 cross-link to carry the data. c3 hole was minor and no issue was opened to track it (checked open-issues.json — no ISS source-tagged WIN-009.c3 exists), so it dropped through the cracks. Standalone-readability is a usability concern rather than a correctness concern, hence minor. Re-raising for the c4 decider to optionally open as an ISS.
holes_found.recommendation: Optionally open a new ISS to add the harmonic quantitative line to detail_evidence. Suggested insertion point: after the bridge sentence ending '...not an independent resonance.' and before 'The robust first harmonic at ~14.3 Hz directly contradicts...'. Suggested text: 'Measured Schumann harmonics span 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz; all four harmonics above 11.7 Hz are observed and directly falsify any cutoff or peak in the inter-harmonic gap.' Minor priority — the cross-link to WIN-008 already carries this argument.

holes_found.description: Verification of landed patches — three patches confirmed correct in substance (ISS-1865/1866 fully clean; ISS-1867 substantively correct but grammatically botched per major hole above). ISS-1865's replacement is verbatim the c3 recommendation and reads naturally in current detail_verdict_text. ISS-1866's bridge sentence is verbatim the c3 recommendation and reads naturally in current detail_evidence. No regressions detected on adjacent text. This is recorded as a 'minor' hole only as a positive verification log entry — no decider action required.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Recorded for audit trail.

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN diff as a meta-argument: WIN-008 and WIN-009 are paired (same physical phenomenon, same evidence cluster, WIN-009 explicitly references WIN-008). The post-hoc paragraph is now templated across both. After the c4 fix, a reader comparing the two WINs sees identical post-hoc framework applied — that consistency itself is a credibility signal. The current grammatical bug breaks this symmetry. Worth keeping the symmetry visible after the fix lands.

kernel_of_truth.description: The ISS-1867 patch had a legitimate goal — replacing inappropriate boilerplate (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, etc., none of which are relevant to a telluric/ELF WIN) with WIN-specific data (MT and ELF spectral literature). The judgment to make the source list per-WIN rather than uniform across all 19 post-hoc WINs is correct and matches the c3 recommendation (data/wins.json:WIN-008.detail_verdict_text shows the parallel construction).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The substantive fix was correct; the implementation introduced new prose damage. Patch correctness has two dimensions — substance (does the new text say the right thing?) and form (is the new text grammatical in context?). The decider's self-apply pipeline currently validates substance well but doesn't proofread find/replace outputs for grammatical fit. The c4 finding is therefore both a per-WIN fix and a pipeline lesson: when the replacement text changes the grammatical class of the spliced-in fragment (here, removing parentheses around an appositive), a manual re-read of the resulting prose is mandatory before push. See monitor/decisions/applied-patches/patches-2026-05-06T07-00-WIN009-WIN050.json for the patch as written.

deeper_analysis: The c3 review surfaced four findings, three of which were converted to issues and patched. Reviewing the post-patch state: ISS-1865 (hardcoded-no-fetch phrasing) — clean. ISS-1866 (MT/Schumann bridge sentence) — clean. ISS-1867 (source-list replacement) — substantively correct but grammatically botched. The find/replace removed parentheses that the surrounding sentence depended on, leaving two parallel noun phrases concatenated with no connector. The other 17 post-hoc-paragraph WINs all preserve the parenthetical pattern, so WIN-009 is a singleton outlier. The fix is mechanical (add parens around the replacement text) and low-risk. The auxiliary minor finding is a c3 hole #4 carry-over — adding a quantitative harmonic line to detail_evidence — which never had an ISS opened against it because c3 graded it minor. Re-flagging for visibility; decider can optionally open it. Lens A advocate mode now finds a credibility-attack angle that didn't exist before the bug: a defender can point to ungrammatical prose in our verdict block as evidence that our post-hoc framework is mechanical boilerplate applied without proofreading. The fix neutralizes this attack. Lens B cross-WIN consistency identifies the WIN-008/WIN-009 pair as the most visible symmetry break — adjacent WINs, same template, one reads correctly and one does not. Lens C quantitative verification has no new mathematical claims to check (the c3 Lorentzian computation still stands; no new numerics introduced by the patches).

citations_verified: Chave & Jones (2012), DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138 — unchanged from c3, still referenced in detail_evidence. Trusted from prior cycles; no web-fetch this run.
WIN-008 anchor (#win008) — still referenced in detail_evidence; build.js-emitted WIN panel ids confirmed valid pattern.
Part 3.1b anchor (#posthoc-standard) — verified in data/sections.json as <span id="posthoc-standard"> just before details element with id="p3-31b".
https://john09289.github.io/predictions — dome home page URL, unchanged from c3.
citations_unchecked: Specific MT/ELF spectral literature implicitly invoked by the new boilerplate phrase 'published MT and ELF spectral literature.' Dome cites no specific DOIs; our text now also doesn't, since the replacement removed the example list. Consider whether the post-hoc paragraph should reference at least one concrete MT spectrum publication to anchor the claim. Not a hole — the post-hoc paragraph is a framework-level statement, not a per-WIN citation requirement.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender, accepting our methodological principles, argues: 'Your post-hoc paragraph on WIN-009 contains ungrammatical text — your review verdict reads as if two boilerplate fragments collided in your build pipeline. If you cannot write coherent prose for your own framework, why should I trust your application of that framework to my WIN? The substantive argument about Schumann harmonics may or may not be right, but the bug in your verdict block reveals that your post-hoc tag is being stamped onto WINs by automation rather than by considered per-WIN analysis. Fix your prose before you tell me my model is mechanical.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix the grammatical bug per hole #1. This neutralizes the credibility-attack angle directly. The defense has no purchase once the prose reads cleanly. Additionally, if the decider opens hole #2 as well (quantitative harmonic line), the WIN-009 detail panel becomes self-sufficient and the cross-link to WIN-008 becomes optional reading rather than load-bearing — further reducing the surface area for 'your post-hoc framework is automation' attacks.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The substantive cross-WIN argument structure (Schumann modes are real, dome 'peaks' inside inter-harmonic minima are misidentifications) remains internally consistent — same physics, same treatment across WIN-001/002/008/009/029. The implementation inconsistency flagged above (WIN-009's broken post-hoc paragraph vs. the 17 other WINs that preserve the parenthetical pattern) is a prose-level break, not an argumentative inconsistency. Once the patch lands, WIN-008 and WIN-009 will read identically in their post-hoc paragraphs, restoring the visual parallel a reader expects when the two WINs explicitly cross-reference each other. WIN-068 carries a slightly different rewrite of the post-hoc paragraph ('Kim & Chang published dataset covering 1991-2016' with no parentheses) — that variant is grammatical and reads naturally, just doesn't follow the boilerplate; not flagged as a defect.

claims_checked: Inter-harmonic minimum between 7.83 and 14.3 Hz (unchanged from c3)
12 Hz has lower power than Schumann peaks on either side (unchanged from c3)
quantitative_verification.computations: No new quantitative claims introduced since c3. The Lorentzian-spectrum computation from c3 (5 modes at 7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz, Q≈4.0-4.5, relative amplitudes 1.0/0.55/0.35/0.22/0.14, sampled 700-1500 in 0.01 Hz steps) found relative power 0.263 at 12 Hz vs 1.055 at 7.83 Hz and 0.637 at 14.3 Hz — confirmed there. Carried forward.
